manufacturer_steps: Check Systems Controls and System Wiring

source_refs.source_quote: Lights Off Off Off No Switched Live to boiler. Check Systems Controls and System Wiring.
No Switched Live to boiler. Check Systems Controls and System Wiring.
Lights On Off Flash Live & Neutral Reversed Incoming Live and Neutral reversed.

possible_causes: Wiring from terminal block to PCB faulty
Wires from inline connector to PCB faulty
Physical blockage to the paddle within the flow switch
manufacturer_steps: Fill system with water and bleed out all air
Check wiring from terminal block to PCB
Check wires from inline connector to PCB

source_refs.source_quote: Lights On Flash Flash Go to Dry-Fire section of the fault finding instructions.

Is the system full of water? Fill system with water and bleed out all air. Is the pump running? Pump or Pump Wiring fault. Is the pump run off the switched live? Pump or Pump Wiring fault. Turn mains off & on. After 5 sec, is there 240V at E? Wiring from terminal block to PCB faulty. Replace PCB. Unplug 5-way PCB connector. Is there continuity between H (run pump from switched live)? Wires from inline connector to PCB faulty. Replace PCB. Disconnect Flow Switch Inline connector. With pump running is there continuity across flow switch? Wires from inline connector to PCB faulty. Replace PCB. Remove flow switch from boiler. Is there a physical blockage to the paddle within the flow switch? Replace Blockage. Is there a blockage in the system? Replace Flow Switch. Replace Blockage.

possible_causes: No gas at gas valve inlet
Lead from PCB to Gas Valve faulty
Condensate trap blocked or water on terminals
Spark or flame detection probe damaged
Wiring from PCB to spark probe & flame detection probe faulty
manufacturer_steps: Check isolation valve and gas supply
Remove Gas Valve & check inlet filter for blockage. Otherwise incorrect gas supply to boiler
Check lead from PCB to Gas Valve
Replace spark or flame detection probe and gaskets
Clean burner or replace as necessary

source_refs.source_quote: 9. Ignition Lockout: The pump, fan, spark generator and gas valve are off. The boiler can only be reset by manually using the reset button.
Lights On Off On Go to Ignition Lockout section of the fault finding instructions.

Is there gas at gas valve inlet? Check isolation valve and gas supply. Reset Lockout. Is there gas flow (check at meter)? Remove Gas Valve & check inlet filter for blockage. Otherwise incorrect gas supply to boiler. Replace Gas Valve. Is there at least 18mbar dynamic at gas valve inlet? Remove 5-way connector from gas valve. Is there 240 Vdc between I & J during ignition? Lead from PCB to Gas Valve faulty. Replace PCB. Is Condensate Trap blocked or water on terminals? Clear blockage and dry sensors. Replace spark or flame detection probe and gaskets. Is spark or flame detection probe damaged? Set spark gap to 3.5mm. Is spark gap between 3 and 4mm? Rectify wiring. Is wiring from PCB to spark probe & flame detection probe OK? Clean burner or replace as necessary. Is the burner blocked or damaged? Replace PCB.

possible_causes: Black stat on flow pipe faulty
Wiring from PCB to thermostats faulty
Combustion chamber door seal damaged or not in place
manufacturer_steps: Check wiring from PCB to thermostats
Replace combustion chamber door seal & trim seal

source_refs.source_quote: Lights Flash Off On Go to Overheat Lockout section of the fault finding instructions.

Disconnect black stat on flow pipe. When flow < 60° C is there continuity across stat? Replace Stat. Reconnect stat. Disconnect fan stat. When fan temp < 60° C is there continurty across stat? Replace Stat. Reconnect stat. Disconnect the larger of the 6-way PCB connectors. Is there continuity across M? Wiring from PCB to thermostats faulty. Disconnect thermistor (red sensor on flow pipe). Is resistance between 0.5KΩ & 20KΩ? Replace thermistor. Is combustion chamber door seal damaged or not in place? Replace combustion chamber door seal & trim seal. Replace PCB.

possible_causes: Wiring from 3-way PCB connector to fan faulty
Wiring from 6-way PCB connector to fan faulty

source_refs.source_quote: Lights Flash Flash On Go to Fan Lockout section of the fault finding instructions.

Unplug 3-way PCB connector & unplug fan. Is there continuity from N to O & from P to Q? Rectify wiring. Unplug the smaller of the 6-way PCB connector. Is there continuity from R to S & from T to U & from V to W? Rectify wiring. Replace fan.
Lights On On On PCB Fault Replace PCB.

possible_causes: Shorts on pump, fan & gas valve
manufacturer_steps: Check for shorts on pump, fan & gas valve. Replace if shorted & replace fuse.

source_refs.source_quote: PCB fuse OK? Check for shorts on pump, fan & gas valve. Replace if shorted & replace fuse.

possible_causes: Wiring from PCB to thermistor faulty
manufacturer_steps: Check wiring from PCB to thermistor

source_refs.source_quote: Lights On Flash On Go to Thermistor section of the fault finding instructions.

Unplug thermistor, Is thermistor resistance between 0.5KΩ & 20KΩ? Replace thermistor. Plug in thermistor, leave 8-way connector unplugged. Is resistance at D between 0.5KΩ & 20KΩ? Wiring from PCB to thermistor faulty. Replace PCB.

safety_warnings.text: This product should be lifted and handled by two people. For recommended hand holds see section 8.3. Stooping should be avoided and protective equipment wom where necessary. Carrying & lifting equipment should be used as required, e.g. when installing in a loft space.

source_refs.source_quote: Lifting - This product should be lifted and handled by two people. For recommended hand holds see section 8.3. Stooping should be avoided and protective equipment wom where necessary. Carrying & lifting equipment should be used as required, e.g. when installing in a loft space.

safety_warnings.text: Check the information on the data plate is compatible with local supply conditions.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning - Check the information on the data plate is compatible with local supply conditions.

safety_warnings.text: Some component parts of this appliance (insulation pads, gaskets and rope seals) are manufactured from man-made mineral fibre. Prolonged or excessive exposure to this material may result in some irritation to the eyes, skin or respiratory tract. It is advisable to wear gloves when handling these items. Irritant dust will only be released from the items if they are broken up or subjected to severe abrasion. In these instances a suitable dust mask and goggles should be worn. Always thoroughly wash hands after installation, servicing or changing components. When disposing of any items manufactured from man-made mineral fibre care must be exercised. If any irritation of the eyes or severe irritation of the skin is experienced seek medical attention.

source_refs.source_quote: Man-made mineral fibre Some component parts of this appliance (insulation pads, gaskets and rope seals) are manufactured from man-made mineral fibre. • Prolonged or excessive exposure to this material may result in some irritation to the eyes, skin or respiratory tract. • It is advisable to wear gloves when handling these items. • Imitant dust will only be released from the items if they are broken up or subjected to severe abrasion. In these instances a suitable dust mask and goggles should be wom. • Always thoroughly wash hands after installation, servicing or changing components. • When disposing of any items manufactured from man-made mineral fibre care must be exercised. • If any imtation of the eyes or severe imitation of the skin is experienced seek medical attention.

safety_warnings.text: Failure to flush and add inhibitor to the system will invalidate the appliance warranty.

source_refs.source_quote: Failure to flush and add inhibitor to the system will invalidate the appliance warranty.

safety_warnings.text: Due to the high efficiency of the boiler a plume of water vapour will be discharged from the flue. This should be taken into account when siting the flue terminal.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: Due to the high efficiency of the boiler a plume of water vapour will be discharged from the flue. This should be taken into account when siting the flue terminal.
NOTE: Due to the high efficiency of the boiler a plume of water vapour will be discharged from the flue. This should be taken into account when siting the flue terminal.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: The ventilation label on the front of the outer case MUST NOT BE REMOVED when the appliance is installed in a compartment or cupboard.

safety_warnings.text: The method of connection to the electricity supply must facilitate complete electrical isolation of the appliance.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: "The method of connection to the electricity supply must facilitate complete electrical isolation of the appliance".

safety_warnings.text: There is no method of isolating the boiler, at the user interface.

source_refs.source_quote: Note! There is no method of isolating the boiler, at the user interface.

safety_warnings.text: FAILURE TO INSTALL THE CONDENSATE DISCHARGE PIPEWORK CORRECTLY WILL AFFECT THE RELIABLE OPERATION OF THE BOILER. The condensate discharge pipe MUST NOT RISE at any point along its length. There MUST be a fall of AT LEAST 2.5° (50mm per metre) along the entire run.

source_refs.source_quote: FAILURE TO INSTALL THE CONDENSATE DISCHARGE PIPEWORK CORRECTLY WILL AFFECT THE RELIABLE OPERATION OF THE BOILER The condensate discharge pipe MUST NOT RISE at any point along its length. There MUST be a fall of AT LEAST 2.5° (50mm per metre) along the entire run.

safety_warnings.text: It is unnecessary to fit an air break in the discharge pipe.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: It is unnecessary to fit an air break in the discharge pipe.

safety_warnings.text: When installing in Loft/Small Compartment access for lifting the boiler from the front can be gained for two people using the lifting points. (Fig. 24).

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: When installing in Loft/Small Compartment access for lifting the boiler from the front can be gained for two people using the lifting points. (Fig. 24).

safety_warnings.text: Drain cocks should be fitted to all system's low points and vents to all high points.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: Drain cocks should be fitted to all system's low points and vents to all high points.

safety_warnings.text: It is important that the pipework does not interfere with the correct fitting of the outer case and a space of 14mm clearance must be left between any vertical pipes and the outer edge of the wall plate.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: It is important that the pipework does not interfere with the correct fitting of the outer case and a space of 14mm clearance must be left between any vertical pipes and the outer edge of the wall plate.

safety_warnings.text: To ensure the correct operation and integrity of the condensate drainage system - Carefully pour approximately I cupful (250ml) of water into the flue products exhaust, at the top of the heat exchanger (Fig. 25a) to ensure a seal is made in the trap.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: To ensure the correct operation and integrity of the condensate drainage system - Carefully pour approximately I cupful (250ml) of water into the flue products exhaust, at the top of the heat exchanger (Fig. 25a) to ensure a seal is made in the trap.

safety_warnings.text: The flue should always be installed with a 3° (I in 20) fall from terminal to elbow, to allow condensate to run back to the boiler.

source_refs.source_quote: IMPORTANT: The flue should always be installed with a 3° (I in 20) fall from terminal to elbow, to allow condensate to run back to the boiler.

safety_warnings.text: Check all measurements before cutting.

source_refs.source_quote: IMPORTANT: Check all measurements before cutting.

safety_warnings.text: When cutting ensure the cut does not interfere with the inner flue support bracket (Fig. 27a).

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: When cutting ensure the cut does not interfere with the inner flue support bracket (Fig. 27a).

safety_warnings.text: This appliance must be earthed.

source_refs.source_quote: WARNING: This appliance must be earthed

safety_warnings.text: When servicing ensure that both the gas and electrical supplies to the boiler are isolated before any work is started.

source_refs.source_quote: IMPORTANT: When servicing ensure that both the gas and electrical supplies to the boiler are isolated before any work is started.
IMPORTANT: When changing components ensure that both the gas and electrical supplies to the boiler are isolated before any work is started.

safety_warnings.text: The boiler cannot be switched off at the boiler, therefore it is important to isolate the electrical supply at the mains fuse.

safety_warnings.text: Remove the trap drain plug and place a vessel underneath to catch the condensate (care should be taken as this could be hot). Clean the trap and refit the drain plug. Check for leaks.

safety_warnings.text: When reassembling always fit new 'O' rings, ensuring their correct location on the spigot. Green "O" rings are used for gas joints and Black "O" rings for water joints. Use Greasil 4000 (Approved Silicone Grease).

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: When reassembling always fit new 'O' rings, ensuring their correct location on the spigot. Green "O" rings are used for gas joints and Black "O" rings for water joints. Use Greasil 4000 (Approved Silicone Grease).

safety_warnings.text: Check for gas tightness after replacing gas valve.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: Check for gas tightness after replacing gas valve.

source_refs.source_quote: IMPORTANT: On refitting the combustion box door check the condition of the combustion box door seals.

source_refs.source_quote: NOTE: The fan is supplied with 325 Vdc.
NOTE: The fan is supplied with 325 Vdc.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Complete Installation and Commissioning sections of Log Book
maintenance_tasks.description: Ensure your installer has completed the Installation and Commissioning sections of the Log Book and hands the Log Book over. The details of the Log Book will be required in the event of any warranty work.

source_refs.section_title: "Benchmark" Installation, Commissioning and Service Record Log Book
source_refs.source_quote: Please ensure that your installer has completed the Installation and Commissioning sections of the Log Book and hands the Log Book over. The details of the Log Book will be required in the event of any warranty work.
Carefully read and complete ali sections of the "Benchmark" Installation, Commissioning and Service Record Log Book that are relevant to the appliance and installation.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Keep Log Book in a safe place
maintenance_tasks.description: Keep the Log Book in a safe place and ensure that the relevant sections are completed at each subsequent regular service visit.

source_refs.section_title: "Benchmark" Installation, Commissioning and Service Record Log Book
source_refs.source_quote: Keep the Log Book in a safe place and ensure that the relevant sections are completed at each subsequent regular service visit.

maintenance_tasks.description: Check the inhibitor concentration after installation, system modification and at every service in accordance with the manufacturer's instructions.
maintenance_tasks.interval: After installation, system modification, and at every service

source_refs.source_quote: It is important to check the inhibitor concentration after installation, system modification and at every service in accordance with the manufacturer's instructions.

maintenance_tasks.description: For reasons of safety and economy, it is recommended that the boiler is serviced annually. Includes cleaning burner, heat exchanger fins, fan compartment, insulation, door seals, electrodes, condensate trap, top of heat exchanger, and checking CO/CO2 ratio.

source_refs.source_quote: For reasons of safety and economy, it is recommended that the boiler is serviced annually.

9. Visually check for debris/damage and clean or replace if necessary the following: a) Burner. b) Heat exchanger fins. c) Fan compartment (Check also for condensate leaks). d) Insulation. e) Door seals-Important: Pay particular attention to the condition of the combustion box door seals. f) Electrodes. g) The condensate trap must be thoroughly cleaned at every service (see section 13.9 for removal). h) Top of heat exchanger.
11. Check CO/CO² ratio at flue sampling point (Fig.4la). See section 4.0.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Instruct user on boiler controls and hand over documents
maintenance_tasks.description: Instruct the user in the operation of the boiler controls. Hand over the User's Operating, Installation and Servicing Instructions and the Log Book, giving advice on the necessity of regular servicing.

source_refs.source_quote: Instruct the user in the operation of the boiler controls. Hand over the User's Operating, Installation and Servicing Instructions and the Log Book, giving advice on the necessity of regular servicing.

maintenance_tasks.description: After changing a component re-commission the boiler where appropriate and check the inhibitor concentration.

source_refs.source_quote: After changing a component re-commission the boiler where appropriate and check the inhibitor concentration (see Section 6.2 and 10.1).

maintenance_tasks.description: Isolate the water circuit and drain the system as necessary. A drain point is located on the heat exchanger manifold at the right hand side of the boiler to enable the heat exchanger to be drained.
maintenance_tasks.interval: Before certain component changes (e.g., thermistor, flowswitch, heat exchanger)

source_refs.source_quote: Isolate the water circuit and drain the system as necessary. A drain point is located on the heat exchanger manifold at the nght hand side of the boiler (Fig. 45) to enable the heat exchanger to be drained.
1. Drain the boiler (see Section 13.1 paragraph 2 & 3).
1. Drain the boiler (see section 13.1 paragraph 2 & 3).
